ticker: Euronext Paris: SAN; NASDAQ ADR: SNY
aging_relevance_reasoning: Sanofi markets therapies in disease areas that are strongly age-associated, including inflammatory/immune-mediated diseases and metabolic disease (e.g., Dupixent/dupilumab for atopic dermatitis and asthma: https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/cder/daf/index.cfm?event=overview.process&ApplNo=761055; Kevzara/sarilumab for rheumatoid arthritis: https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/cder/daf/index.cfm?event=overview.process&ApplNo=761037; Lantus/insulin glargine for diabetes mellitus: https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/cder/daf/index.cfm?event=overview.process&ApplNo=021081). It also has late-stage neurology development targeting immune pathways implicated in neuroinflammation (tolebrutinib Phase 3 in multiple sclerosis: https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT04410978). However, Sanofi is not publicly positioned as a company directly developing therapeutics to target aging biology as an indication.

existing_longevity_assets.indication: Atopic dermatitis; asthma (additional labeled indications exist)
existing_longevity_assets.relevance: Targets type 2 inflammation pathways (IL-4/IL-13 signaling per label), making it potentially relevant to aging biology via chronic inflammation/altered immune signaling (analysis).
IL-6 receptor blockade (per label) is relevant to inflammatory signaling networks that overlap with age-related chronic inflammation (analysis).

Addresses metabolic dysregulation (a major driver of age-related morbidity); overlaps conceptually with nutrient-sensing/metabolic aging pathways but is not a direct geroscience intervention (analysis).

BTK inhibition in MS is relevant to immune-mediated neuroinflammation; neuroinflammatory modulation overlaps with aging hallmarks related to intercellular communication and chronic inflammation (analysis).

recent_acquisitions.relevance: Autoimmune/type 1 diabetes-focused acquisition; relevant to immunomodulation of age-associated immune dysfunction and chronic inflammation (analysis).
recent_acquisitions.source: Sanofi press release: "Sanofi to acquire Provention Bio" (2023)

recent_acquisitions.relevance: mRNA platform acquisition; platform could enable rapid development of vaccines/therapeutics applicable to age-associated infectious disease risk and potentially other indications (analysis).
recent_acquisitions.source: Sanofi press release: "Sanofi to acquire Translate Bio" (2021)

recent_acquisitions.relevance: Immunology pipeline acquisition (BTK inhibitor programs); relevant to modulation of immune/inflammatory signaling that intersects with aging hallmarks (analysis).
recent_acquisitions.source: Sanofi press release: "Sanofi to acquire Principia Biopharma" (2020)

recent_acquisitions.relevance: Immuno-oncology cytokine biology acquisition; relevance to immune modulation (analysis).
recent_acquisitions.source: Sanofi press release: "Sanofi to acquire Synthorx" (2019)

acquisition_pattern.preferred_stage: Late clinical-stage to commercial-stage boltons, plus occasional platform acquisitions (analysis; examples include Provention Bio, Translate Bio, Principia Biopharma, Synthorx—sources needed as cited in recent_acquisitions).
focus_areas: Immunology/autoimmune (inferred from Provention Bio and Principia Biopharma acquisitions; sources needed as cited in recent_acquisitions)
Platform technologies (mRNA) (inferred from Translate Bio acquisition; sources needed as cited in recent_acquisitions)
acquisition_pattern.recent_trend: Recent M&A shows emphasis on immunology/autoimmune assets and enabling platforms (Translate Bio, Principia Biopharma, Provention Bio, Synthorx—sources needed as cited in recent_acquisitions).
